codeReview.summary: 31 well-structured tests covering all 15 validation assertions (VAL-SMARTREC-001 through VAL-SMARTREC-015) plus 11 additional edge-case tests. The worker correctly identified and handled the dedup logic, fake timer requirements for date-fns, and the component's null-render condition. Mocking of ProgressTracker and StreakTracker is thorough with a flexible setupMocks() helper. The test file follows existing project conventions (jest.mock, fireEvent, beforeEach/clearAllMocks). All 358 tests pass, lint passes clean.

issues.description: VAL-SMARTREC-011 cursor test for 'default cursor when no module' only tests cards that DO have a module (checks that pointer-cursor cards have .recommendation-action). Since all current recommendation types in the source always attach a module, there is no code path producing a card without a module. The test is effectively a no-op for the 'default cursor' branch but does not cause false positives. This matches the source code reality — no recommendation type omits the module field — so the test is harmless.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md mentions using userEvent.setup() for realistic clicks, but the worker had to switch to fireEvent due to fake timer conflicts. This is a real pattern: jest.useFakeTimers() and userEvent don't play well together (userEvent has internal async delays). The AGENTS.md guidance should note this caveat when fake timers are active.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows the worker initially tried userEvent.setup() for VAL-SMARTREC-010 click test, got a timeout failure, and switched to fireEvent.click() which passed. AGENTS.md line 'Use userEvent.setup() for realistic clicks' doesn't mention the fake-timer incompatibility.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md mentions wrapping components with ToastProvider when they use @/components/ui/toast, but SmartRecommendations.js does not actually import or use toast. The worker correctly omitted the ToastProvider wrapper. The AGENTS.md note may be slightly inaccurate for this component.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SmartRecommendations.js imports only React, date-fns/format, ProgressTracker, StreakTracker, and dashboard.css — no toast import. AGENTS.md says: '@/components/ui/toast is imported by ModuleDetail, SmartRecommendations, VideoCard — components using it need ToastProvider wrapper'.

summary: Pass. All 15 validation assertions are covered by well-designed tests with appropriate mock setups. The worker demonstrated good understanding of the component's recommendation generation logic, including deduplication, priority sorting, and date-dependent behavior. One non-blocking observation about the cursor 'default' test being effectively untestable due to source code design (all recommendation types always include a module). Two shared-state observations about AGENTS.md accuracy: (1) userEvent vs fake timers conflict, (2) SmartRecommendations doesn't actually use toast.
